channel.title: RNS News — Equity & Markets Insight
channel.description: Summarised UK regulatory news with priority levels

item.title: BP p.l.c. — BP flags lower Q2 upstream production of 2,170-2,220mboe/d but sharply higher oil, gas and refining realisations, with net debt falling to $22-23bn.
item.description: [HIGH] BP Q2 2026 trading statement ahead of full results on 4 August 2026 ; contains inside information covering estimates vs 1Q 2026 . Reported upstream production 2,170-2,220mboe/d (1Q: 2,339mboe/d ), down on seasonal maintenance in the Gulf of America and Middle East disruption; oil 1,420-1,450mboe/d, gas & low carbon 750-770mboe/d. Realisations to lift earnings vs 1Q: oil +$1.8 to 2.1bn , refining m

item.title: Taylor Wimpey PLC — Taylor Wimpey appoints Sarah Warby as an independent Non-Executive Director from 2 October 2026, as Clodagh Moriarty steps down on 31 August 2026.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Sarah Warby appointed independent Non-Executive Director with effect from 2 October 2026 , joining the Nomination and Governance Committee and Remuneration Committee. Warby is currently Chief Customer Officer at Nando's UK&I and a NED at MONY Group plc , with over 30 years in customer and brand, previously CMO at J Sainsbury plc and CEO of Lovehoney Ltd . Clodagh Moriarty to step down from the Boa

item.title: Mony Group PLC — Mony Group non-executive director Sarah Warby to join Taylor Wimpey plc board as a non-executive director from 2 October 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Sarah Warby , a non-executive director of Mony Group PLC , will be appointed a non-executive director of Taylor Wimpey plc with effect from 2 October 2026 . Routine external-directorship disclosure under UKLR 6.4.9(2) with no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: IntegraFin Holdings PLC — IntegraFin's Transact platform funds under direction hit £85.5bn, up 23% year-on-year, with a sixth consecutive quarter of net inflows above £1bn.
item.description: [HIGH] Transact funds under direction (FUD) reached £85.5bn at quarter end, up 23% year-on-year (Q3 FY25: £69.5bn ), on strong global equity markets and continued net inflows. Net inflows of £1.0bn in Q3 FY26 (Q3 FY25: £1.2bn ), a 6th consecutive quarter above £1bn, equal to 5.2% of opening FUD annualised. Gross inflows of £2.6bn , 5% ahead of prior year (Q3 FY25: £2.5bn); over 2,000 net new clients adde

item.title: boohoo Group PLC — Debenhams Group reports continued positive trading through June and July, with GMV growing year on year, margins up and returns down.
item.description: [HIGH] Debenhams Group (formerly Boohoo Group ) reports continued positive trading through June and July , ahead of today's AGM, per CEO Dan Finley . GMV continues to grow year on year , margins are up and returns are down ; hot weather especially benefited the Debenhams brand. Cites two upgrades to guidance in nine months ; PLT (Young Fashion) returned to growth and profitability . Expects net debt mate

item.title: Construction Partners Inc — Construction Partners has completed its acquisition of Tulsa-based asphalt and construction business Ellsworth Construction, expanding into the Tulsa and Oklahoma City markets.
item.description: [HIGH] Construction Partners, Inc. (NASDAQ: ROAD) has acquired Ellsworth Construction, LLC , a Tulsa-headquartered asphalt manufacturing and construction business, expanding its Oklahoma platform into the Tulsa and Oklahoma City metro markets. Ellsworth operates a hot-mix asphalt plant in Broken Arrow plus a permitted plant site in Greater Oklahoma City , providing paving, sitework and utility services,

item.title: Workspace Group PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Workspace Board rejects the Saba Materials (updated presentation and Q&A published 7 July 2026 ) ahead of the AGM on 23 July 2026 , urging shareholders to vote FOR Resolutions 1-20 and AGAINST Saba's Resolutions 21-26 . Board characterises Saba's plan as a high-risk, 'stealth managed wind-down' — originally demanding a full wind-down within 12 months (Jan 2026) — that would position Workspace as a

item.title: Tesco PLC — Corporate update
item.description: Tesco Corporate Treasury Services PLC has admitted EUR500,000,000 3.750% Notes due 2034 (ISIN XS3437614228) to trading on the London Stock Exchange Main Market. Routine debt admission-to-trading notice under the FCA Prospectus Rules — no impact on Tesco trading or guidance.

item.title: Tullow Oil plc — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Tullow Oil plc has agreed to terminate its Kenyan royalty payments and back-in right with Gulf Energy Limited in exchange for $9m additional consideration on the sale of Tullow Kenya B.V. to Auron Energy E&P Limited . The deal accelerates near-term cash; proceeds will be used to strengthen the balance sheet. Completion and receipt of funds expected no later than 17 July 2026 . Further to the July

item.title: Trekor — Corporate update
item.description: Trekor has submitted a Detailed Project Description (DPD) for the Yellowhead copper project to the BC Environmental Assessment Office , advancing it to the next phase of the provincial EA process. Proposed open-pit mine near Vavenby, British Columbia is designed to produce 178 million pounds of copper annually over a 25-year mine life . Milestone informs the upcoming Readiness Decision and Process

item.title: Prologis, Inc. — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Prologis, Inc. published a new investor presentation pressing the case for a possible combination with SEGRO plc , following engagement with SEGRO shareholders and SEGRO's own presentation the prior day — this is not a firm offer under Rule 2.7 and there is no certainty any offer will be made. Prologis argues it brings a superior, better-capitalised data centre platform — a 5.8GW secured/advanced

item.title: Playtech PLC — Trading update
item.description: Trading significantly ahead of market expectations , driven by excellent US performance and continued strength in Mexico, Colombia and certain European markets; H1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA expected to be over €155m . FY2026 (year to 31 December 2026) Adjusted EBITDA now guided to at least €270m , significantly above consensus of €219m (range €205m–€225m across 7 analysts). Americas momentum led by the

item.title: Londonmetric Property PLC — Trading update
item.description: AGM trading update : £7.6bn triple net lease (NNN) portfolio (£8bn group) continues to perform well; occupancy up to 98.3% from 97.7% at the start of the financial year, average lease length 17 years . Since 1 April 2026, £139m of investment transactions and £6.7m pa of additional rent from 72 asset management initiatives — 49 rent reviews added £4.3m (avg uplift 16% ; logistics +23% ), 23 letting

item.title: Harbour Energy PLC — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Harbour Energy has completed its acquisition of substantially all UK subsidiaries of Waldorf Energy Partners Ltd and Waldorf Production Ltd for $163m . Completion follows receipt of all regulatory approvals and full and final settlement of all creditors' claims against the acquired Waldorf subsidiaries. Final consideration is post customary adjustments and reflects a transaction effective date of

item.title: easyJet PLC — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Castlelake, L.P. confirms it is still considering a possible offer for the entire issued and to be issued share capital of easyJet plc , first announced on 5 July 2026 — a Rule 2.4 statement, no firm intention to bid yet. Follows the 10 July 2026 announcement by easyJet and Apollo regarding Apollo's own possible offer, setting up a potential contested situation for easyJet . Castlelake is a global

item.title: Hollywood Bowl Group PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Rachel Addison , Senior Independent Director of Hollywood Bowl Group plc , appointed Non-Executive Director and Chair of the Audit Committee of Rosebank Industries plc , effective today . Routine external-directorship disclosure under Listing Rule 6.4.9 — no impact on Hollywood Bowl's trading or guidance.

item.title: Berkeley Group Holdings PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Richard Dakin , Independent Non-Executive Director of Berkeley Group Holdings , appointed an Independent Non-Executive Director of Schroder Real Estate Investment Trust Limited with immediate effect. Joins SREI's Audit, Management Engagement and Nomination Committees, and becomes Chair of the Management Engagement Committee after SREI's AGM on 22 September 2026 . Routine external-directorship disc

item.title: Vodafone Group PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Vodafone withdraws Resolution 7 from its 2026 AGM — the re-election of Hatem Dowidar as a Director — following his resignation from the Board with immediate effect, announced on 10 July 2026 . Any votes already cast on Resolution 7 will not apply; the withdrawal does not affect the validity of the Notice, proxy form or votes on the remaining resolutions, whose numbering is unchanged. Procedural fo

item.title: Rathbones Group Plc — Share buyback
item.description: Rathbones Group Plc has completed its £20m share buyback programme, launched 17 June 2026 . Purchased 939,315 Ordinary Shares for cancellation at an average £16.221275 per share. Programme concluded as Investec's interests reached the IW&I completion levels: 29.90% voting and 41.25% economic interest. Post-cancellation issued capital: 90,489,844 Ordinary Shares (with voting rights) and 17,481,868

item.title: Schroder European Real Estate Investment Trust Ltd — Trading update
item.description: Property portfolio independently valued at €185.1m as at 30 June 2026 (31 March 2026: €192.6m ), a €7.5m or -3.9% fall over the quarter. Decline reflects weaker investment demand, particularly for secondary offices, amid macro uncertainty, inflation and interest-rate concerns, driving further outward yield pressure and more conservative assumptions. Largest markdown at St Cloud, Paris (office): do

item.title: Me Group International PLC — Interim results
item.description: H1 2026 (six months to 30 April 2026 ): group revenue marginally up 0.3% to £154.3m , EBITDA up 7.1% to £57.0m (margin stable at 36.9% ), but PBT down 3.8% to £32.7m ; diluted EPS 6.48p , down 3.9%. Laundry the key growth driver — Wash.ME vending revenue up 16.3% to £54.8m , now 38.4% of group vending revenue and 53.9% of group EBITDA; 499 net new laundry machines installed, on track for c. 1,300

item.title: Plus500 Ltd — Trading update
item.description: Plus500 Limited H1 2026 trading update: Customer Income up 24% YoY to $460.8m (H1 2025: $371.5m), a five-year record for a six-month period; revenue up 12% YoY to $462.9m (H1 2025: $415.1m), a three-year high. EBITDA up 1% YoY to $187.5m (H1 2025: $185.1m), a 41% margin , held back by deliberately higher customer-acquisition spend and FX cost headwinds. FY 2026 revenue and EBITDA reconfirmed in li

item.title: Derwent London PLC — Debt financing
item.description: Derwent London has signed a new £100m unsecured revolving credit facility with Handelsbanken plc , a first-time lender to the Group. Initial five-year term, with two one-year extension options and a £50m accordion option, each subject to lender consent. Margin and financial covenants in line with the Group's existing bank facilities; CFO Damian Wisniewski cited continued banking-market support and

item.title: Great Portland Estates PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Andrew White , Development Director, to leave Great Portland Estates plc later this year after more than thirteen years with the Company. White joined in 2013 and led delivery of over 2.5 million sq ft of prime development, generating more than £400 million of development surpluses across projects including Rathbone Square, Hanover Square and 30 Broadwick Street. He will remain during a transition

item.title: MJ Gleeson PLC — Trading update
item.description: Cavendish upgrades forecasts on MJ Gleeson's FY26 trading update , citing land discipline driving a materially improved cash flow outlook. FY26E group revenue raised 9.9% to £401.8m , reflecting Homes completions of 1,968 in line with the statement. FY27E and FY28E revenue lifted 4.3% and 1.4% respectively on a stronger volume and pricing mix. Divisional EBIT increased 10.0% in FY26E and 9.7% in F

item.title: Zigup PLC — Annual results
item.description: ZIGUP plc ( LSE:ZIG ) to host a live fireside chat via Investor Meet Company on Monday 13 July at 14:30 BST , covering its Full Year Results published the previous day. Presenting: CEO Martin Ward and CFO Rachel Coulson ; open to existing and potential shareholders, with questions submittable via the platform. Routine investor-access notice — no impact on trading or guidance; contains no new finan

item.title: Wizz Air Holdings PLC — AGM notice
item.description: Wizz Air Holdings Plc has published the notice of its 2026 Annual General Meeting , to be held at 3:45 p.m. CEST on 5 August 2026 at the Crowne Plaza Geneva , Switzerland; notice and form of proxy are available on the investor relations website. Routine administrative notice with no impact on trading or guidance; Restricted Share Notices will be sent to Non-Qualifying National shareholders regardi

item.title: Vodafone Group PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Vodafone notes that e& (Emirates Telecommunications Group Company PJSC) has agreed to sell its entire shareholding in Vodafone to Vega , an acquisition vehicle wholly owned by the Niel family group . The relationship agreement dated 11 May 2023 between Vodafone and e& has been terminated . Hatem Dowidar , e&'s nominee director, has resigned from the Board with immediate effect .

item.title: Shell PLC — Annual results
item.description: Shell plc announces final results of its exchange offers to swap outstanding unregistered notes of Shell Finance US Inc. for like-for-like new registered notes, fully guaranteed by Shell. Total principal validly tendered and accepted for exchange was $6,298,101,000 ; settlement expected on 13 July 2026 . Routine debt registration exercise — a like-for-like note exchange with no impact on Shell's e

item.title: Schroder European Real Estate Investment Trust Ltd — Interim results
item.description: Schroder European Real Estate Investment Trust plc posted interim results to 31 March 2026 alongside a proposal for a managed wind down of the trust. Board and manager judge a wind down and return of capital the best route to maximise shareholder value, having reviewed various options; process expected to take two to three years to allow asset management initiatives ahead of sales. Proposal requir

item.title: Paragon Banking Group PLC — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Paragon Banking Group PLC confirms completion of the sale of its vehicle and fleet subsidiary . Provided article text carries no deal value, buyer name or financial impact — details of consideration and P&L effect are not stated in the scraped content. Full-year results for the twelve months to 30 September 2026 to be released on Tuesday 1 December 2026 . Enquiries directed to CFO Richard Woodman

item.title: Old Mutual Ltd — Corporate update
item.description: Old Mutual to migrate its secondary Zimbabwe listing from the Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE) to the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX) , resolving the suspension of trading in its shares that has persisted since June 2020 . Migration aims to restore trading freedom for Zimbabwean shareholders; VFEX is a USD -denominated exchange, lowering currency risk versus the ZSE. Board cites VFEX scale: aver

item.title: Mountview Estates PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Mountview Estates P.L.C. has published its 2026 Annual Report and Accounts (year ended 31 March 2026 ) and the Notice of AGM; routine Listing Rule 6.4.1R disclosure with no impact on trading or guidance — no new financials disclosed. 2026 AGM to be held at Norton Rose Fulbright LLP, 3 More London Riverside, on Wednesday, 12 August 2026 at 11.00 a.m. ; shareholders may submit questions in advance b

item.title: Moonpig Group PLC — Annual results
item.description: Moonpig Group plc posting its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 30 April 2026 and Notice of AGM to shareholders on 10 July 2026 ; documents submitted to the National Storage Mechanism. Follows the 25 June 2026 announcement of annual results — this is a routine administrative filing with no impact on trading or guidance. AGM to be held at 10:00am BST on Wednesday 16 September 2026 at th

item.title: Me Group International PLC — Interim results
item.description: ME Group International plc (LSE: MEGP ), the instant-service vending equipment group, will host a live investor presentation via Investor Meet Company at 11.00am GMT on Monday 13 July 2026 . Presentation covers the Group's Interim Results for the six months ended 30 April 2026 , given by Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki ; questions can be submitted pre-event or during the session. Routine notice o

item.title: Picton Property Income Limited — Acquisition / deal
item.description: LondonMetric Property Plc and Schroder Real Estate Investment Trust Limited (the Consortium) have sweetened their non-binding, indicative all-share possible offer for Picton Property Income Limited , raising the SREIT exchange ratio to 0.894 new SREIT shares (from 0.881 ) plus an unchanged 0.190 LondonMetric shares per Picton share. Revised terms imply 77.0 pence per Picton share, valuing Picton a

item.title: Johnson Service Group PLC — Trading update
item.description: Johnson Service Group PLC remains on track to deliver its targeted adjusted operating margin of at least 14.0% in 2026 , with the Board expecting another year of progress and margin improvement. H1 group revenue expected in line with prior year at £258.0m (2025: £257.5m); Workwear £74.0m (2025: £72.1m) and HORECA £184.0m (2025: £185.4m). Group organic revenue softened to approximately (0.7%) : Wor

item.title: HSBC Holdings PLC — Corporate update
item.description: Routine all-employee share plan disclosure: on 9 July 2026 HSBC granted conditional awards over 273,474.84948 ordinary shares (US$0.50 each) under the HSBC International Employee Share Purchase Plan . Awards carry no performance conditions or clawback given the all-employee nature of the plan; 1,111,378,343 shares remain available under the 10% plan mandate limit. No impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Harbour Energy PLC — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Completed the acquisition of substantially all subsidiaries of Waldorf Energy Partners Ltd and Waldorf Production Ltd in the UK for $163m . Deal follows receipt of all regulatory approvals and full and final settlement of all creditors' claims against the acquired Waldorf subsidiaries. Consideration is post customary adjustments and reflects a transaction effective date of 1 January 2025 . Harbour

item.title: MJ Gleeson PLC — Trading update
item.description: FY2026 (year ended 30 June 2026 ) concluded in line with market expectations — Adjusted Group Profit Before Tax to land within company-compiled consensus of £10.1m (range £8.9m–£11.0m ); audited results due 15 September 2026 . Gleeson Homes completed 1,968 homes , up 9.8% (FY2025: 1,793 ), including first-ever partnership completions of 320 units and 301 private multi-unit sales; net reservation r

item.title: easyJet PLC — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Apollo (Apollo Management X / Apollo Global Management) has reached agreement in principle on key financial terms of a possible cash offer of £7.15 per share for the entire issued and to be issued share capital of easyJet , valuing the fully diluted equity at approximately £5.7 billion . This is a Rule 2.4 statement only — not a firm intention to make an offer under Rule 2.7; there is no certainty

item.title: Carnival PLC — Corporate update
item.description: Carnival Cruise Line held the steel-cutting ceremony at Fincantieri 's Monfalcone shipyard for Carnival Destiny , the first of three vessels in its next-generation Ace Class , with delivery scheduled for summer 2029 . Two further Ace-Class ships are planned for 2031 and 2033 ; part of a five-ship growth programme through 2033 that also includes fourth and fifth Excel-class ships in 2027 and 2028 .

item.title: Bridgepoint Group PLC — Acquisition / deal
item.description: Cavendish updates forecasts on Bridgepoint's acquisition of Kayne Anderson Real Estate (KARE) for an upfront EV of $1.39bn . Deal adds a scaled $22bn US real estate platform, establishing real estate as Bridgepoint's fifth investment vertical . Broadens product offering and lifts pro forma AUM to c$117bn , with greater contribution from recurring management fees enhancing earnings quality. Cavendi

item.title: Berkeley Group Holdings PLC — Board / management change
item.description: Richard Dakin , Independent Non-Executive Director of Berkeley , appointed Independent Non-Executive Director of Schroder Real Estate Investment Trust Limited with immediate effect; joins its Audit, Management Engagement and Nomination Committees, and becomes Chair of the Management Engagement Committee after SREI's AGM on 22 September 2026 . Routine disclosure under UK Listing Rule 6.4.9(2) — no

item.title: Babcock International Group PLC — Debt issuance
item.description: Babcock International Group PLC to issue £250m of five-year bonds carrying a fixed coupon of 5.125% , its first bond issue for seven years . Proceeds to refinance the £300m bond maturing October 2026 ; net proceeds also for general corporate purposes and growth investment. Company cites strong demand on the back of its balance sheet and leading positioning in defence and nuclear , allowing pricing

item.title: United Rentals Inc — Trading update
item.description: United Rentals will report Q2 2026 results after market close on Wednesday, 22 July 2026 , with a conference call to follow at 8:30 a.m. ET on Thursday, 23 July 2026 . Call hosted by CEO Matt Flannery and CFO Ted Grace ; routine scheduling notice with no financial data disclosed.

item.title: Tullow Oil plc — Board / management change
item.description: Garrett Soden , a Non-Executive Director of Tullow Oil plc , has been appointed a Non-Executive Director of Polar Resources AS , listed on Euronext Growth . Routine UKLR 6.4.9 external-directorship disclosure with no impact on Tullow's trading or guidance.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Inc — Trading update
item.description: Steel Dynamics, Inc. (NASDAQ/GS: STLD ) will release second quarter 2026 financial results after market close on Monday, 20 July 2026 , with an earnings call at 11:00 a.m. EDT on Tuesday, 21 July 2026 . Call hosted by chairman and CEO Mark D. Millett , CFO Theresa E. Wagler and president and COO Barry Schneider ; scheduling notice only, no financial figures or guidance disclosed.

item.title: SLM Corporation — Trading update
item.description: Sallie Mae ( SLM Corporation , Nasdaq: SLM ) will release second-quarter 2026 financial results after market close on Thursday, 23 July 2026 , with a webcast at 5:30 p.m. ET . Scheduling notice only — no financial data disclosed. US-listed private student lender.

item.title: CME Group Inc — Corporate update
item.description: CME Group has launched the CME Group Agriculture Index , a broad-based, monthly -updated price benchmark tracking the aggregate performance of the global farm economy. The index integrates futures prices across five sectors — Grains, Oilseeds, Livestock, Dairy and Lumber — with a methodology that neutralises delivery-month price differences and applies market weightings. Positioned as a single-pla
